one_line_value_proposition: Clinician-reviewed remote monitoring and virtual ward platform with 15 years of NHS COPD deployment and an explicit NHSE ICB £125k funding endorsement.
why_it_matters_locally: Clinitouch enables NHS organisations to stand up virtual COPD wards that reduce unplanned admissions and free up bed capacity. It supports 24/7 monitoring hub models and integrates with HL7 FHIR. Spirit Health is explicitly named in NHSE winter 2024 COPD technology funding.
target_problem_statement: High unplanned COPD admissions, long hospital stays, inadequate post-discharge monitoring and limited virtual ward capacity.
evidence_summary: Two peer-reviewed observational studies (Ghosh et al. 2016, 2018) showing 67.5% reduction in unscheduled admissions and 484% ROI, a peer-reviewed economic evaluation of a COVID-19 digital ward (Swift et al. JMIR 2024) and an NHSX grey-literature case study. All key authors have declared Spirit Health affiliations. No independent RCT for Clinitouch in COPD has been identified. NICE HTE19 reviewed and recommended with conditions.
evidence_strength_rationale: NICE recommends self-management use during evidence generation, but the COPD evidence located by NICE is two before-after studies and a retrospective study, with no COPD RCT. Reported admission/CAT improvements are promising, but comparator details are weak; PR use is research-only.

case_studies.title: Leicester COPD programme — admission reduction and ROI
case_studies.setting: Leicestershire Partnership NHS Trust, urban COPD population, 12 months
case_studies.outcome: Admissions fell from 3.13 to 1.02 per patient (p<0.001). 67.5% reduction in unscheduled admissions. Net saving £622k from £52k service cost. £2,278 saving per patient.
case_studies.caveat: Observational study. Authors have declared Spirit Health affiliations. Combined Clinitouch + clinical health coaching + specialist nurse intervention — outcomes reflect the full model, not technology alone.
case_studies.source: Ghosh et al., Br J Healthcare Management 2016, DOI: 10.12968/bjhc.2016.22.3.123

case_studies.title: LLR ICS virtual ward — bed days saved
case_studies.setting: NHS Leicester, Leicestershire and Rutland ICS, COVID-19 digital ward, 2020–2021
case_studies.outcome: 1,103 bed days saved. £529,719 net financial savings. Sensitivity analysis: 78.4% simultaneous reduction in effects and cost increase required to reach break-even.
case_studies.caveat: Peer-reviewed but authors include Spirit Health staff (declared COI). COVID-19 context — not directly transferable to standard COPD.
case_studies.source: Swift et al., JMIR Formative Research 2024, DOI: 10.2196/47441, PMID: 38349716

live_sites: LLR ICS (16 pathways), Leicestershire Partnership NHS Trust (since 2013), Royal Devon University Healthcare NHS FT, Bradford District and Craven, 20+ international partners in 15 countries

named_sites.name: Royal Devon University Healthcare NHS FT

patients_covered_note: System-wide deployment across LLR ICS. 20+ international partner organisations. Patient count not publicly confirmed for individual deployments.
dtac_note: NHS Digital Playbook confirms DTAC completion. Central completion may be over 3 years old — updated DTAC form (April 2026) may require refresh. Confirm with Spirit Health before commissioning.
gdpr_note: GDPR compliant. Specific data hosting location not confirmed in public sources.
target_patients: Adults with COPD (any severity). Platform supports 15+ other long-term conditions including heart failure, frailty, diabetes and asthma.
exclusions: Requires active clinician monitoring — not suitable for organisations that cannot staff dashboard review.
onboarding_detail: Co-design with clinical teams to configure pathway parameters, questionnaire sets and alert thresholds before deployment. Community/ward staff support patient initial setup.
supplier_wrap: Spirit Health works directly with NHS organisations to design, configure and deploy clinical pathways. Training for all clinical staff. Supports 24/7 monitoring hub setup.
local_wraparound_detail: Clinical team required to review dashboard and respond to alerts. 24/7 staffing needed for virtual ward model. Pathway design must be completed before deployment — Spirit Health supports this process but local clinical commitment is essential.
training_note: Training provided to all clinical staff. Platform designed to be kept simple.
monitoring_note: Active remote management model — clinical team or contracted 24/7 hub must review dashboard. This is not a self-management-only tool.
escalation_note: Clinical teams configure alert thresholds and escalation protocols. Spirit Health supports hub setup.
operating_hours_caveat: Virtual ward model requires staffed monitoring capability. Confirm monitoring hours and escalation protocols with Spirit Health before deployment.
implementation_prerequisites: Clinical team with capacity to review dashboard
Pathway design completed with Spirit Health
Bluetooth monitoring devices procured and supplied to patients
indicative_price_text: National framework pricing available. Per-patient-per-month model with indicative range of £25–£50 depending on volume and contract term. Implementation and training costs typically quoted separately. Contact supplier for current schedule.
service_wrap_description: Standard contract includes platform access, implementation support, clinical pathway design consultancy, training programme, 24/7 technical support, and monthly reporting. Enhanced tier with dedicated clinical informatics support and custom EPR integration available.
service_wrap_note: Spirit Health provides co-design, configuration and implementation support. Standard contract includes platform access, implementation support, clinical pathway design consultancy, training programme, 24/7 technical support, and monthly reporting. Spirit Health Foundation offers unlimited free licences for LMICs.
contract_note: NHS Digital Marketplace and direct contract routes available.
procurement_notes: Available via NHSE ICB COPD Digital Technology Fund. G-Cloud and direct procurement routes.
expected_benefit_note: 67.5% reduction in unscheduled COPD admissions in Leicester programme; 1,103 bed days saved in LLR ICS virtual ward (ROI evidence carries conflict-of-interest caveat). More generally, benefit is most likely when combined with effective clinical monitoring and response; real-world data are more encouraging than RCT evidence alone for admission avoidance.
tariff_considerations: Telemonitoring may be funded under virtual ward programme allocations. Commissioners should check local virtual ward funding scope. Potential to support Best Practice Tariff compliance for COPD where applicable. Long-term condition management costs sit with commissioner. Leicester programme generated £622k net saving from £52k service cost but reflects a combined clinical and digital intervention — model full service wrap locally.
provider_income_note: Supports providers in delivering virtual ward targets and managing COPD cohorts at scale. May reduce some emergency and inpatient activity over time. Consider pathway-level financial modelling to align commissioner and provider incentives.
roi_note: Supplier provides detailed ROI modelling based on NHS deployment data. Liverpool evaluation suggests potential net savings when admission avoidance is factored against monitoring costs, but this is observational. Earlier literature cited 484% system ROI (Ghosh 2018) with declared COI; treat as directional. Commissioners should model using local cost and activity data.
nhse_125k_note: Clinitouch is explicitly named as eligible for NHSE ICB £125k COPD technology funding (August 2024 deadline). Spirit Health can support ICB applications — contact clinitouch@spirit-health.com.
demo_notes: Contact Spirit Health for a configured demo environment.
source_summary: NICE HTG736, Ghosh et al. BJHM 2016 and 2018, Swift et al. JMIR Formative Research 2024, NHSX/NHS Transformation Directorate Digital Playbook, Spirit Health website.
confidence_note: Content based on publicly available information. Key evidence carries declared conflict-of-interest caveats. Verify all claims with Spirit Health before use in procurement documentation.

clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: Leicestershire Partnership NHS Trust — COPD in urban setting, 12 months
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Admissions fell from 3.13 to 1.02 per patient (p<0.001). 67.5% reduction in unscheduled admissions. Net saving £622k from £52k service cost. £2,278 saving per patient.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Observational. Combined intervention (Clinitouch + clinical health coaching + specialist nurse) — outcomes reflect the full model, not technology alone.
clinical_evidence_detailed.coi_note: Authors have Spirit Health affiliations — declared conflict of interest.
clinical_evidence_detailed.data_quality_note: Authors have Spirit Health affiliations with declared conflicts of interest. No independent RCT identified.

clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: Leicestershire Partnership NHS Trust — COPD programme
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: 484% return on investment to the health system from Clinitouch remote monitoring in COPD.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Authors have Spirit Health affiliations — declared conflict of interest.
clinical_evidence_detailed.coi_note: Authors have Spirit Health affiliations — declared conflict of interest.
clinical_evidence_detailed.data_quality_note: Treat ROI claims with appropriate caution given declared COI. No independent RCT identified for Clinitouch in COPD.

clinical_evidence_detailed.authors: Swift J, O'Kelly N, Brierley-Jones C et al.
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: 1,103 bed days saved; £529,719 net financial savings. Sensitivity analysis: 78.4% simultaneous reduction in effects and cost increase required to reach break-even.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: COVID-19 context — not directly transferable to standard COPD. Authors include Spirit Health staff (declared COI).
clinical_evidence_detailed.coi_note: Authors include Spirit Health staff — declared conflict of interest.
clinical_evidence_detailed.data_quality_note: Spirit Health staff authorship with declared COI. COVID-19 virtual ward context.

clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: 50% reduction in COVID-19 re-admissions for patients discharged with Clinitouch monitoring.
clinical_evidence_detailed.source_label: NHS Transformation Directorate Respiratory Digital Playbook

monitoring_model: Active remote monitoring with clinician dashboard and automated alerts.
minimum_conditions_for_success: Dedicated clinical monitoring team, respiratory clinical lead, minimum 200 patient cohort.
cyber_notes: ISO 27001 certified. Cyber Essentials Plus held.

technical_integrations.device_integration: Pulse oximetry, spirometry, blood pressure via connected devices

context_of_use.population: Adults with moderate-severe COPD requiring remote monitoring
context_of_use.therapeutic_purpose: Active remote monitoring, symptom tracking, clinical alerts, exacerbation prevention
context_of_use.nice_scope: NICE HTE19 (HTG736) — recommended during 3-year evidence generation period
